title: artemisia borealis f. wormskioldii
intro: Artemisia borealis f. wormskioldii, also known as Wormskjold's arctic mugwort, is a subspecies of the arctic mugwort plant (Artemisia borealis) native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Greenland. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Wormskjold's arctic mugwort include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Artemisia borealis f. wormskioldii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
